purpose: Introduce the first language and framework reference profiles for Ananta so new-project and project-evolution flows can use curated real-world reference systems instead of relying only on generic language knowledge.
goal: Provide a first practical reference-pack system using Keycloak for Java and Ananta itself for Python backend and Angular frontend so Ananta can better guide project creation and project evolution in those ecosystems.
success_criteria: The system can recognize and expose initial reference profiles for Java, Python and Angular.
Reference profiles are used as guidance and retrieval sources, not as blind copy templates.
New-project and project-evolution flows can choose suitable reference packs explicitly.
Reference usage remains bounded, explainable and auditable.

tasks.title: Reference profile model define
acceptance_criteria: A reference profile model is defined with fields such as language, framework, project_type, reference_source, strengths, limitations and intended usage.
The model is small enough for a first implementation.
The model can grow later without breaking initial profiles.

tasks.title: Reference usage boundary define
acceptance_criteria: The system explicitly treats references as pattern and architecture guidance rather than blind copy sources.
The design explains what kinds of reuse are intended such as structure, conventions, layering and test patterns.
The design avoids presenting references as canonical truth for every project.

tasks.title: Reference selection strategy define
acceptance_criteria: A first strategy exists for selecting references by language and project type.
The strategy supports new-project and project-evolution flows.
The strategy remains deterministic enough for explanation and debugging.

tasks.title: Reference provenance and audit visibility define
acceptance_criteria: The system can show which reference profile influenced a task or recommendation.
Reference influence is visible in traces or read models where useful.
The design supports auditability later.

tasks.title: Java Keycloak reference profile define
acceptance_criteria: A first Java reference profile exists based on Keycloak.
The profile documents why Keycloak is useful such as large Java codebase, security-heavy architecture and enterprise backend patterns.
The profile also documents limitations so it is not treated as the universal Java template.

tasks.title: Python Ananta backend reference profile define
acceptance_criteria: A first Python reference profile exists based on the Ananta backend.
The profile highlights strengths such as orchestration, API layering, governance-aware backend structure and local runtime integration.
The profile states clearly for which Python project types it is suitable.

tasks.title: Angular Ananta frontend reference profile define
acceptance_criteria: A first Angular reference profile exists based on the Ananta frontend.
The profile highlights strengths such as admin-heavy UI structure, workflow surfaces and Angular application organization.
The profile documents where it fits and where it does not.

tasks.title: Starter reference catalog expose
acceptance_criteria: The initial three profiles are available through a catalog or read-model surface.
The catalog is understandable for users and operators.
The catalog distinguishes language, framework and intended usage.

tasks.title: Reference retrieval entry points define
acceptance_criteria: The system defines how reference material enters retrieval and prompt assembly.
The design is bounded and avoids dumping entire repositories into context.
Relevant slices can be selected by architecture, file type or use case.

tasks.title: Pattern extraction categories define
acceptance_criteria: A small set of pattern categories is defined such as project structure, module organization, configuration style, test structure, API layout and security conventions.
The categories are reusable across the three initial profiles.
The categories support meaningful retrieval.

tasks.title: Reference chunking and indexing strategy add
acceptance_criteria: Reference repositories can be indexed in a way suitable for bounded retrieval.
The indexing retains provenance and enough metadata to explain why a chunk was selected.
The strategy avoids uncontrolled context bloat.

tasks.title: Reference recommendation reason output add
acceptance_criteria: When Ananta uses a reference profile, it can explain why it chose it.
The explanation includes language, project type and one or more pattern reasons.
The explanation is compact and useful.

tasks.title: New-project reference selection flow add
acceptance_criteria: The new-project flow can choose or recommend one of the starter reference profiles.
The recommendation can depend on language and project type.
The chosen reference remains visible to the user.

tasks.title: Blueprint and work-profile integration for references add
acceptance_criteria: Reference profile choice can influence blueprint or work-profile selection where useful.
The integration stays explicit and explainable.
Reference usage does not silently override product logic.

tasks.title: Reference-aware project skeleton guidance add
acceptance_criteria: New-project output can use selected reference profiles to suggest structure, modules and conventions.
The output remains guidance and not a blind clone.
The result is more concrete than generic language-only advice.

tasks.title: Project-evolution reference matching add
acceptance_criteria: The project-evolution flow can infer or recommend a matching starter reference profile for the current repository.
Matching uses at least language and coarse project shape.
The result can be inspected by the user.

tasks.title: Reference-aware review and evolution hints add
acceptance_criteria: During project evolution, Ananta can use reference profiles to suggest structure, conventions or likely missing patterns.
Hints are framed as recommendations, not absolute judgments.
The output is actionable and not vague.

tasks.title: Reference-aware diagnostics for mismatch cases add
acceptance_criteria: The system can indicate when a selected or inferred reference profile fits poorly.
Mismatch feedback helps prevent forcing bad patterns onto the project.
The output remains understandable.

tasks.title: Reference influence boundaries define
acceptance_criteria: The system defines where references may influence recommendations and where they may not bypass governance.
Reference material cannot override approval, policy or security constraints.
The boundary is explicit in implementation and docs.

tasks.title: Reference source quality rules define
acceptance_criteria: The system documents why the chosen starter references are considered high-signal enough for initial use.
The rules can later help decide which additional references are acceptable.
The selection process remains curated rather than random.

tasks.title: Reference usage audit markers add
acceptance_criteria: Relevant operations can record which reference profile was used.
The marker is visible in diagnostics or audit where useful.
The feature supports later explainability.

tasks.title: UI and CLI visibility for reference profiles add
acceptance_criteria: Users can see the selected or recommended reference profile in UI and relevant CLI flows.
The presentation is compact and useful.
Users are not surprised by hidden reference influence.

tasks.title: Starter reference profile docs write
acceptance_criteria: Documentation exists for the three starter reference profiles.
The docs explain strengths, fit and limitations of each profile.
The docs are useful for users and maintainers.

tasks.title: Golden path examples for each starter reference add
acceptance_criteria: There is one concrete example for Java/Keycloak, Python/Ananta backend and Angular/Ananta frontend usage.
The examples are tied to new-project or project-evolution flows.
The examples are reproducible.

tasks.title: Tests for reference selection and bounded usage add
acceptance_criteria: Tests protect reference selection logic and bounded retrieval behavior.
The suite verifies that references are used as guidance rather than uncontrolled full-context injection.
The suite covers at least one flow per starter profile.

tasks.title: Roadmap for later additional references isolate
acceptance_criteria: Further possible references are documented separately from the first rollout.
The first release stays focused on Keycloak and Ananta as starter references.
The roadmap does not bloat the MVP.

tasks.title: Reference profile model define
acceptance_criteria: A reference profile model is defined with evolvable fields.

tasks.title: Reference usage boundary define
acceptance_criteria: Reference usage is guidance-only and never blind copy.

tasks.title: Reference selection strategy define
acceptance_criteria: Selection strategy is deterministic and explainable.

tasks.title: Reference provenance and audit visibility define
acceptance_criteria: Reference influence is visible via traces/read models.

tasks.title: Java Keycloak reference profile define
acceptance_criteria: A curated Java/Keycloak profile with fit+limits exists.

tasks.title: Python Ananta backend reference profile define
acceptance_criteria: A curated Python/Ananta-backend profile with fit scope exists.

tasks.title: Angular Ananta frontend reference profile define
acceptance_criteria: A curated Angular/Ananta-frontend profile with fit scope exists.

tasks.title: Starter reference catalog expose
acceptance_criteria: Starter catalog exposes language/framework/use-case clearly.

tasks.title: Reference retrieval entry points define
acceptance_criteria: Reference retrieval entry points are bounded and explicit.

tasks.title: Pattern extraction categories define
acceptance_criteria: Reusable cross-profile pattern categories are defined.

tasks.title: Reference chunking and indexing strategy add
acceptance_criteria: Chunking/indexing preserves provenance and avoids context bloat.

tasks.title: Reference recommendation reason output add
acceptance_criteria: Reference choice reasons are concise and useful.

tasks.title: New-project reference selection flow add
acceptance_criteria: New-project flow selects/recommends starter references visibly.

tasks.title: Blueprint and work-profile integration for references add
acceptance_criteria: Reference choice influences profiles explicitly and explainably.

tasks.title: Reference-aware project skeleton guidance add
acceptance_criteria: Skeleton guidance is concrete but never blind-cloned.

tasks.title: Project-evolution reference matching add
acceptance_criteria: Evolution flow can infer/recommend matching references.

tasks.title: Reference-aware review and evolution hints add
acceptance_criteria: Evolution hints are actionable recommendations, not judgments.

tasks.title: Reference-aware diagnostics for mismatch cases add
acceptance_criteria: Mismatch diagnostics prevent forced bad-pattern adoption.

tasks.title: Reference influence boundaries define
acceptance_criteria: References cannot bypass policy/approval/security boundaries.

tasks.title: Reference source quality rules define
acceptance_criteria: Curated quality rules for acceptable references are documented.

tasks.title: Reference usage audit markers add
acceptance_criteria: Audit markers identify where references influenced outputs.

tasks.title: UI and CLI visibility for reference profiles add
acceptance_criteria: UI/CLI show selected reference profile clearly.

tasks.title: Starter reference profile docs write
acceptance_criteria: Docs for three starter profiles include strengths and limits.

tasks.title: Golden path examples for each starter reference add
acceptance_criteria: One reproducible golden-path example per starter profile exists.

tasks.title: Tests for reference selection and bounded usage add
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover bounded retrieval and guided-reference behavior.

tasks.title: Roadmap for later additional references isolate
acceptance_criteria: Post-MVP references are isolated in a non-blocking roadmap.

notes: Die vorherige Struktur enthielt nur groups/tasks ohne task_track-Rollups.
Milestones, tasks-Flattening und konsistente Summary-Counter wurden ergänzt.
Der Track ist jetzt validator-kompatibel und für sequenzielle Abarbeitung vorbereitet.

summary_notes: Der Track bleibt referenz-orientiert und nutzt Quellen als Guidance statt Copy-Template.
Die neue Normalisierung erhält groups als semantische Struktur und ergänzt task_track-Pflichtfelder.
Mit execution_stage_summary ist die Bündelreihenfolge operativ transparent.
Kritischer Pfad bleibt explizit sichtbar und kann priorisiert abgearbeitet werden.

end_summaries.title: Analyseergebnis
items: Die aktive todo.json war semantisch gut strukturiert (groups), aber nicht task_track-validator-kompatibel.
Pflichtfelder wie milestones, tasks, risk_scale und tasks_status_summary fehlten.

end_summaries.title: Korrekturen
items: Ein vollständiges task_track-Modell wurde ergänzt (milestones, tasks, status/priority/risk Rollups, critical_path).

end_summaries.title: Verbesserungen/Erweiterungen
items: Flattened tasks enthalten group_id zur Rückverfolgbarkeit zwischen operativer und fachlicher Struktur.
analysis_checks und summary_notes wurden zur transparenten Wartbarkeit ergänzt.

end_summaries.title: Status am Ende
items: Aktueller Umsetzungsstand: 11 todo, 0 in_progress, 0 blocked, 15 done (57.69%).
